zh.lifeSummary: 當Anna Catherina Wolf於1600年5月11日出生在德國賀瑟可萊斯 本海姆格洛瑙 貝 本海姆時，她的父親Johann Christof Wolf24歲，母親Anna Barbara Mehbreys15歲。她於大約1624年與Johann Martin Stober在德國巴登貝爾可薩赤 卡爾茲盧斯塔福特結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於1668年在德國巴登-爾特波格藍德可萊斯 卡爾茲盧斯圖斯塔福特享年68歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Anna Catherina Wolf于1600年5月11日出生在德国賀瑟可萊斯 本海姆格洛瑙 貝 本海姆时，她的父亲Johann Christof Wolf24岁，母亲Anna Barbara Mehbreys15岁。她于大约1624年与Johann Martin Stober在德国巴登貝爾可薩赤 卡爾茲盧斯塔福特结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。她去世于1668年在德国巴登-爾特波格藍德可萊斯 卡爾茲盧斯圖斯塔福特享年68岁。

nameMeanings.name: Wolf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German, English, Dutch, and Danish: from a short form of the various ancient Germanic compound names with the first element wolf ‘wolf’, or a byname or nickname with this meaning, or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a wolf. The wolf was native throughout the forests of Europe, including Britain, until comparatively recently. In ancient and medieval times it played an important role in ancient Germanic mythology, being regarded as one of the sacred beasts of Woden. The surname of German origin is also found in many other parts of Europe, e.g. in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Poland, Hungary, Czechia, Slovakia, Slovenia, and Croatia, often as a German translation of local equivalents. In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Hungarian Farkas , Czech and Slovak Vlk , Slovenian Volk , Ukrainian and Slovenian Vovk , Slovenian, Croatian, and Serbian Vuk , and also Czech, Slovenian, Croatian, Slovak, and Hungarian Volf . In part, this is a Gottscheerish (i.e. Gottschee German) surname, originating from the Kočevsko region in Lower Carniola, Slovenia (see Kocevar ). Compare De Wolf , Wolfe , Wolff , Woolf , Woulfe , and Wulf .

Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the Yiddish male personal name Volf meaning ‘wolf’, which is associated with the Hebrew personal name Binyamin (see Benjamin ). This association stems from Jacob's dying words ‘Benjamin shall ravin as a wolf: in the morning he shall devour the prey, and at night he shall divide the spoil’ (Genesis 49:27). Compare Volf .
Irish: variant of   Woulfe  .

variants.name: Volkov
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Russian Igor, Sergey, Oleg, Vladimir, Boris, Grigoriy, Sergei, Aleksandr, Anatoliy, Arkadiy, Emiliya, Evgeni, Vladislav.  Jewish Baruch, Naum, Ruvim.
Russian: patronymic from the old personal name or nickname Volk, from volk ‘wolf’.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): habitational name from any of the places called Volkovo or Volki, now in Belarus, which in turn are derived from Russian volk ‘wolf’.

variants.name: Littlewolf
meanings: Native American (also Little Wolf): translation into English of a personal name such as Chippewa Ma'iingaans, from a diminutive of ma'iingan ‘wolf’, or Lakota Sioux Šuŋgmanitu Čikala, composed of šuŋgmanitu ‘wolf’ and čikala ‘little’. See also Wolf 4.
Native American (Cheyenne): from a mistranslation into English of the Cheyenne personal name O'kohomoxhaahketa ‘Little Coyote’ (see Littlecoyote ).

History: O'kohomoxhaahketa (Anglicized as e.g. Ohcumgache) was the name of a Northern Cheyenne chief (c. 1820–1904) who, in 1878, led a heroic escape of his people from confinement in OK back to MT, the Cheyenne homeland. He is known in English as Little Wolf. He had two sons, but the name Little Wolf was carried on as a surname by his nephew.

variants.name: Wilf
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Emanuel, Gittel, Ilan.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): from a personal name, a Yiddish dialect variant of   Wolf  .

variants.name: Woelfel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Ernst, Erwin, Erna, Florian, Kurt, Othmar, Ulrike.
German (mainly Wölfel): from a pet form of the personal name Wolf or any of the compound names beginning with this element.

variants.name: Wolfanger
meanings: German: variant of Wolffanger ‘wolf catcher’, an occupational name for a trapper who caught wolves (see Wolf , Fanger ).

variants.name: Farkas
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Laszlo, Tibor, Attila, Sandor, Zoltan, Gabor, Geza, Janos, Istvan, Miklos, Bela, Csaba.  Jewish Arie, Chaim, Moshe, Liat, Mayer, Zev, Ari, Asher, Eliezer, Faigy, Fishel, Mendel.

Hungarian: from the old personal name Farkas, derived from farkas ‘wolf’. It is also a nickname derived from the same word. The surname Farkas is also found in Romania and Slovakia (see also 2 below).

Slovak, Czech, Serbian, Croatian, Slovenian, and Rusyn (from Vojvodina in Serbia) (Farkaš): from a Slavicized form of Hungarian farkas ‘wolf’, used either as a nickname or a personal name; or, in part, an adaptation of the cognate Hungarian surname Farkas (see 1 above).

variants.name: Lupu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Romanian Cornel, Florin, Mihai, Petru, Traian, Viorel, Alejandro, Corina, Costica, Elena, Ilie.
Romanian: nickname from lupu ‘wolf’ (from Latin lupus). It may also be from the old personal name Lupu with the same meaning.
Jewish (from Romania): translation (see above) of the personal name   Wolf   ‘wolf’.

variants.name: Volf
meanings: Czech, Slovenian, Croatian, Slovak, and Hungarian: from German   Wolf   1, which was often used as a translation into German of corresponding Slavic nicknames or surnames. Compare   Bolf  .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Wolf   2.

variants.name: Vuk
meanings: Slovenian: nickname from vuk, a dialect variant of volk ‘wolf’. Compare Wolf .
Croatian, Serbian, and Bosniak: from the Croatian and Serbian personal name Vuk ‘wolf’; or a nickname from a Croatian, Serbian, and Bosnian word with this meaning (compare 1 above).

variants.name: Wolfe
meanings: English, German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Wolf   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this. This is also a common spelling in Ireland (see also   Woulfe  ), particularly in counties Cork and Limerick, while in Germany this surname is very rare.
Americanized form of various other surnames meaning ‘wolf’, e.g. Hungarian   Farkas  , Czech and Slovak   Vlk  , Slovenian and other Slavic   Volk  ,   Vovk  , and   Vuk   (see   Wolf  ).

nameMeanings.name: Anna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

variants.name: Anne
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

en.lifeSummary: When Anna Catherina Wolf was born on 11 May 1600, in Gronau bei Bensheim, Kreis Bensheim, Grand Duchy of Hesse, Germany, her father, Johann Christof Wolf, was 24 and her mother, Anna Barbara Mehbreys, was 15. She married Johann Martin Stober about 1624, in Staffort, Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden, Germany. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died in 1668, in Staffort, Stutensee, Karlsruhe, Baden-Württemberg, Germany, at the age of 68.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Anna Catherina Wolf est née le 11 mai 1600, à Gronau bei Bensheim, Kreis Bensheim, Hessen, Allemagne, son père, Johann Christof Wolf, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Anna Barbara Mehbreys, avait 15 ans. Elle a épousé Johann Martin Stober environ 1624, à Staffort, Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden, Allemagne. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée en 1668, à Staffort, Stutensee, Landkreis Karlsruhe, Baden-Württemberg, Allemagne, à l’âge de 68 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Anna Catherina Wolf am 11. Mai 1600 in Gronau bei Bensheim, Kreis Bensheim, Großherzogtum Hessen, Deutschland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Johann Christof Wolf 24 und ihre Mutter Anna Barbara Mehbreys 15. Sie hat um 1624 in Staffort, Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden, Deutschland Johann Martin Stober geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Sie starb 1668 im Alter von 68 Jahren in Staffort, Stutensee, Landkreis Karlsruhe, Baden-Württemberg, Deutschland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Anna Catherina Wolf è nata il il 11 maggio 1600 nel Gronau bei Bensheim, Kreis Bensheim, Hessen, Germania, sua padre, Johann Christof Wolf, aveva 24 anni e sua madre, Anna Barbara Mehbreys, aveva 15 anni. Lei sposato Johann Martin Stober circa 1624, nel Staffort, Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden, Germania. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì nel 1668, nel Staffort, Stutensee, Landkreis Karlsruhe, Baden-Württemberg, Germania, all'età di 68 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Anna Catherina Wolfが1600年5月11日にドイツヘセンクレイス ベンシェイムグロナウ ベイ ベンシェイムで生まれた時，父Johann Christof Wolfは24歳，母Anna Barbara Mehbreysは15歳であった。約1624年にドイツバデンベズイルクサムト カルルスルヘスタフォートでJohann Martin Stoberと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼女は1668年にドイツバデン-ウuルッテンバーグランドクレイス カルルスルヘストゥテンセスタフォートで68歳で死去した。

ko.Staffort, Stutensee, Karlsruhe, Baden-Wü: 독일 바데-wu르테버그 랜드크레이스 칼스루헤 스투텐시 스태포트
독일 바데-wu르테버그 랜드크레이스 칼스루헤 스투텐시 스태포트
ko.lifeSummary: Anna Catherina Wolf(이)가 1600년 5월 11일 독일 헤센 크레이스 벤셰임 그로나 브이 벤셰임에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Johann Christof Wolf(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anna Barbara Mehbreys(은)는 15세였다. 그녀는 대략 1624년 독일 바든 베지르크샘트 칼스루헤 스태포트에서 Johann Martin Stober(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1668년 독일 바데-wu르테버그 랜드크레이스 칼스루헤 스투텐시 스태포트에서 68세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Anna Catherina Wolf nasceu em 11 de maio de 1600, em Gronau bei Bensheim, Kreis Bensheim, Hessen, Alemanha, seu pai, Johann Christof Wolf, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Anna Barbara Mehbreys, tinha 15 anos. Ela casou-se com Johann Martin Stober aproximadamente 1624, em Staffort, Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden, Alemanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ela faleceu em 1668, em Staffort, Stutensee, Landkreis Karlsruhe, Baden-Württemberg, Alemanha, com 68 anos.

ru.Staffort, Stutensee, Karlsruhe, Baden-Wü: стаффорт, стутенси, ландкрис карлсрух, баден-вuрттембирг, Германия
стаффорт, стутенси, ландкрис карлсрух, баден-вuрттембирг, Германия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Anna Catherina Wolf родилась 11 мая 1600 г. в местности гронау би беншим, крис беншим, Гессен, Германия, её отцу по имени Johann Christof Wolf было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Anna Barbara Mehbreys, 15. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Johann Martin Stober около 1624 г. в местности стаффорт, безирксэмт карлсрух, баден, Германия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она умерла в 1668 г. в местности стаффорт, стутенси, ландкрис карлсрух, баден-вuрттембирг, Германия в возрасте 68 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Anna Catherina Wolf nació el 11 de mayo de 1600, en Gronau bei Bensheim, Kreis Bensheim, Hessen, Alemania, su papá, Johann Christof Wolf, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Anna Barbara Mehbreys, tenía 15 años. Se casó con Johann Martin Stober aproximadamente 1624, en Staffort, Bezirksamt Karlsruhe, Baden, Alemania. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos.
